mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.58% as of 2005-08-15 suggests easing bias
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend indicates robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,544k as of 2005-08-15 reflects strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with month-over-month PAYEMS growth over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation coefficient falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 529,766M as of 2005-07-15 provides baseline for contraction detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where RSAFS contracts >2% MoM, or Rank IC > -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, indicating strong corporate profitability and positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows more than 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.20 as of 2005-07-15 reflects mid-cycle expansion
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong supply-side momentum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in periods of sustained INDPRO growth >0.5% MoM are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. When both rise simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days; when both fall, returns are negative. The interaction captures demand-side momentum that single-series models miss.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,567k and RSAFS at 529,766M as of 2005-07-15 provide baseline for joint momentum
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: Joint HOUST+RSAFS growth signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, the real policy rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation. This regime produces positive equity returns over 5 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges in equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 259.05 and FEDFUNDS at 1.58% as of 2005-08-15 suggest negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes show zero or negative correlation with the real rate spread, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and foreshadows consumer spending weakness and earnings downgrades. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2005-08-15 is below 4.0% threshold, indicating labor market strength
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in periods where UNRATE > 4.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC > -0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration and foreshadows earnings downgrades across the equity market. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations in anticipation of slower profit growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% in any quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,220.49B as of 2005-04-15 provides baseline for growth rate calculation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following quarters of GDPC1 growth < 2.0% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC > -0.07 over any 3-year rolling window.
